# Transcrição: How_to_Give_&_Receive_Compliments_WITHOUT_Deflecting

- **Fonte:** How_to_Give_&_Receive_Compliments_WITHOUT_Deflecting
- **Duração:** 02:08
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:08]

We all have that one friend who just can't accept a compliment gracefully. Let me share with you a story about one of my friends and to protect his identity, let's just call him Daniel Yao. I'll tag his social media below to protect his identity. If you are a deflector, this is what you tend to do. Whenever I tell Dan something positive like, hey Dan, thanks for always being early. He brushes it off by saying things like, oh that's a part of my job man. Or, I could always be earlier. Here's a simple solution. While it's important for Dan to get better at accepting compliments, I've also found a way to make it easier for him. By ending my compliments with an open-ended question. Here's the technique in action. Instead of saying, Dan, thanks for always staying back late and helping me answer emails. I would say, Dan, I appreciate you staying late to answer emails. What were the key issues our clients were facing? Do you see what I did there? The moment Dan starts answering my open-ended question, the compliment has already been accepted, which reduces the awkwardness for those who are uncomfortable with direct praise. Here's an extra tip for serial deflectors for the Dan's that exist in your world. God, they're so annoying. Remember, compliments framed as observational statements are less likely to be deflected. So instead of saying something like, Dan, you're the best speaker at the conference, to which he would most likely deflect and say, no, no, no, no, no, Vin, you're way better. And look, he's right. I am better. But that's besides the point. Watch what happens when I make the compliment observational. Dan, I saw the audience nodding and writing things down. It was super clear they got a lot out of your presentation. Can you see how with observational compliments, they're harder to deflect? Now, just for funsies, I want everyone to at DZ Yell in the comment section and let's leave Dan a compliment because with our powers combined, there is no way he can beat us. He can't possibly deflect them all. Here's Dan, now compliment him.
